metadata.description: Back at port. The cargo delivered, the heat settling. Time to count the coin and see what comes next.

content_hints.description: Kessler Station, Tier 2 berth. A grimy commercial port on the edge of Hegemonic space. Your berth is sandwiched between a water hauler and a decommissioned mining rig. The air tastes of ozone and fried circuitry. Nobody here asks questions. That's the point.
content_hints.read_aloud: You dock at Kessler and the tension bleeds out of the ship like pressure from an airlock. The job is done. The Memish product is off-loaded to the buyer's people within the hour -- three quiet figures in unmarked jumpsuits who move the crates with practiced efficiency and say nothing.

content_hints.description: The fence's berth. A converted shipping container fitted out as an office, its walls lined with display cases of merchandise whose provenance you'd rather not know. The fence sits behind a desk made from a salvaged bulkhead, counting your payment with the unhurried precision of someone who has all the time in the sector.

npcs.name: Sable
npcs.dialogue: Clean run. Mostly. I heard about the corvette -- word travels fast on the relay. The Knives are pleased, which means I'm pleased, which means you get the full cut. Don't spend it all on fuel.
npcs.notes: Sable (she/her). Runs a fencing operation out of Kessler. Pragmatic, well-connected, and the closest thing the crew has to a reliable payoff contact. She takes 10% off the top and considers it a bargain.

loot.name: Job payment
loot.description: Cred-sticks loaded and verified. The Ashen Knives pay on delivery.

content_hints.description: The Dead Circuit, Kessler's only bar. Bolted stools, a countertop that's more stain than surface, and a bartender droid whose voice modulator is stuck on 'sarcastic.' The kind of place where you go to not be found, and everyone there is doing the same thing.
event_triggers: Citani's holo-image materializes uninvited at your table. 'Good work. The Knives have moved you up to +2. But the Hegemony logged your transponder at Warren gate. You've got heat. I'd recommend laying low -- or taking another job to build up goodwill elsewhere. I have options, if you're interested.'
